title: Documentation Checkpoint Example Fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind bug

**Description**

The [documentation](https://podman.io/getting-started/checkpoint.html) checkpoint example does not work. Checkpointing itself fails. I am using Ubuntu 21.10 and CRIU 3.14. Is there something I can do?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd`

2. `sudo podman container checkpoint <container_id>`

**Describe the results you received:**
CRIU fails to checkpoint the container with this error:
```
ERRO[0000] container is not destroyed                   
ERRO[0000] criu failed: type NOTIFY errno 0
log file: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/1f313238e3e1fd5a20cb61bc70dae75347fffca5e81681a823ef1707a59ac0d6/userdata/dump.log 
Error: `/usr/sbin/runc checkpoint --image-path /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/1f313238e3e1fd5a20cb61bc70dae75347fffca5e81681a823ef1707a59ac0d6/userdata/checkpoint --work-path /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/1f313238e3e1fd5a20cb61bc70dae75347fffca5e81681a823ef1707a59ac0d6/userdata 1f313238e3e1fd5a20cb61bc70dae75347fffca5e81681a823ef1707a59ac0d6` failed: exit status 1
```

In the logs, you can see:
```
(00.006797) Collecting mappings (pid: 59051)
(00.006799) ----------------------------------------
(00.006867) Error (criu/files-reg.c:1371): Can't lookup mount=448 for fd=-3 path=/usr/local/apache2/bin/httpd
(00.006875) Error (criu/cr-dump.c:1247): Collect mappings (pid: 59051) failed with -1
(00.006891) Unlock network
(00.006892) Running network-unlock scripts
(00.006893)     RPC
(00.008596) Unfreezing tasks into 1
(00.008604)     Unseizing 59051 into 1
(00.008613)     Unseizing 59064 into 1
(00.008733)     Unseizing 59065 into 1
(00.008853)     Unseizing 59067 into 1
(00.009010) Error (criu/cr-dump.c:1764): Dumping FAILED.
```

I'm attaching the complete log in [dump.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8403688/dump.log). I am also attaching the output of `podman inspect` here in [inspect.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8403699/inspect.txt)

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the container to be checkpointed. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
Version:      3.2.1
API Version:  3.2.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs

        FUSE library version 3.10.3
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/vagrant/.local/share/containers/storage

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/impish,now 3.2.1+ds1-2ubuntu3 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes. Although I am reporting for version 3.2.1 which is in the Ubuntu repositories, I have tried version 4.0.3 and it results in the same.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Virtual machine running on VirtualBox on AMD Ryzen 9 3900X. I've also tried the suggestion to do `loginctl enable-linger 1000` but it did not make a difference.

comments.body: The Ubuntu kernel has non-upstreamed kernel patches for overlayfs which breaks CRIU. Try it on a non Ubuntu system and it should work.
@adrianreber thank you! I tried with Fedora and it works! 👍
